extraction.one_line_summary: HINDLEY Jai won the Blockhaus sprint from six-rider group after YATES crashed out with knee injury and LOPEZ miraculously limited losses to 1:46 to retain pink
tactical_patterns: INEOS deployed PORTE as sole domestique with 12km remaining, forcing rivals to attack with no teammates rather than controlling pace.
CARAPAZ attacked but BALDE/LANDER immediately followed, creating stalemate that allowed ALMEIDA/POZZOVIVO to rejoin rather than selection.
HINDLEY positioned perfectly into final corner while BALDE lost crucial meters, demonstrating descent positioning matters even on summit finishes.

rider_signals.YATES Simon: negative: abandoned before Blockhaus with strapped knee from Etna crash, confirming that injury eliminated him as GC threat
rider_signals.LOPEZ Juan Pedro: positive: limited losses to 1:46 despite losing contact with 12km remaining, revealing unexpected climbing durability for non-climber
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: negative: first GC casualty on Blockhaus with mechanical at critical moment, exposing BORA team depth issues
rider_signals.PORTE Richie: neutral: worked as last INEOS domestique but inability to pace group longer suggests diminished climbing strength compared to previous years
rider_signals.HINDLEY Jai: positive: superior positioning into final corner delivered stage win from six-man group, showing tactical sharpness beyond pure climbing
